summary: Iteration 14 full-stack review: 7 new customer proxy endpoints + /data-freshness + inventory locations filter verified via pytest (11/11 passed). UI verified on /, /customers, /re-order, /inventory, /footfall, /ceo-report. All new/rewritten pages render correctly once fetches settle (15-36s first load). 2 real bugs found: 'Made with Emergent' badge still visible (CSS rule cannot override inline !important), and Customers Churn Rate KPI shows nonsense 165836.96%.

ui_bugs.component: Customers page — Churn Rate KPI card
ui_bugs.issue: Displays '165836.96%' — clearly a calculation error (likely churned/active ratio × 100 with wrong denominator or percentage multiplied twice). Total Customers=92, so a realistic churn rate should be single-digit or low double-digit %. Please review Customers.jsx churn calculation.
ui_bugs.selector: Customers page 6th KPI card (rightmost) with label 'Churn Rate'

ui_bugs.issue: Badge still visible in bottom-right corner on every page. Root cause: the <a id='emergent-badge'> element in /app/frontend/public/index.html has inline style `display: inline-flex !important;` which WINS over the external CSS `display: none !important;` in index.css (per CSS cascade: inline !important > external !important for equal specificity). Fix: either (a) remove/comment out the <a id='emergent-badge'> block in public/index.html, or (b) add a small JS snippet in index.html / App.js that calls `document.getElementById('emergent-badge')?.remove()` on mount.

ui_bugs.component: Customers page — KPI card data-testid attributes
ui_bugs.issue: Review_request referenced testids 'kpi-total', 'kpi-new', 'kpi-returning', 'kpi-repeat', 'kpi-avg-spend', 'kpi-churn' but none are present in DOM. KPI cards render visually (Total 92, New 15, Returning 73, Repeat 4, Avg Spend KES 8,712, Churn Rate ...) but without data-testid they are harder to regression-test. Consider adding data-testid="kpi-total" etc.

integration_issues.issue: Customers, Inventory, Re-Order take 11-18s to finish loading; CEO Report takes ~36s on first visit (multiple upstream fetches, some with upstream 500 retries). Not a bug per se — upstream BI latency — but UX could benefit from per-section skeleton loaders instead of a single full-page 'Loading…' spinner so users see partial data sooner.

backend_customer_endpoints.top_customers: 200 OK, list (empty due to upstream 500 — expected)
backend_customer_endpoints.churned_customers: 200 OK, list (empty due to upstream 500 — expected)
backend_customer_endpoints.customers_by_location: 200 OK, list (23 POS locations observed in UI)
backend_customer_endpoints.data_freshness: 200 OK, all 6 keys present (last_sale_date=2026-04-22, sla_hours=6, etl_cadence='Every 6 hours')

customers_page.kpi_grid_rendered: 6 cards: Total=92, New=15, Returning=73, Repeat=4, AvgSpend=KES 8,712, ChurnRate=165836.96% (BUG)

ceo_report_page.note: Collection header still exists in at least one table; review_request said REMOVED only from Top 10 Best SOR. Likely still in Bottom 10 or another section — visual inspection needed to confirm it was removed specifically from Top 10 Best SOR. Headers observed: COUNTRY, TOTAL SALES, VS LM, VS LY, ORDERS, UNITS, AVG BASKET, RETURNS, LOCATION, RANK, STYLE NAME, SUBCATEGORY, UNITS SOLD, TOTAL SALES KES, AVG PRICE KES, % OF SOLD, CURRENT STOCK, % OF STOCK, VARIANCE, SOR, STYLE, STOCK, COLLECTION, RETURN RATE, FOOTFALL, CONVERSION, SALES / VISITOR

emergent_badge.root_cause: inline style display:inline-flex!important on #emergent-badge in public/index.html beats external CSS display:none!important

action_items: Fix Customers Churn Rate calculation (currently returning 165836.96% — likely a decimal vs percent conversion bug in Customers.jsx)
Remove/hide 'Made with Emergent' badge via JS removal or by editing public/index.html <a id=emergent-badge> block — CSS alone cannot beat inline !important
Add data-testid="kpi-total", "kpi-new", "kpi-returning", "kpi-repeat", "kpi-avg-spend", "kpi-churn" to Customers page KPI cards for testability
(Optional UX) Replace full-page 'Loading…' on heavy pages (Customers, CEO, Inventory) with per-section skeleton loaders — CEO Report first-load is 36s
critical_code_review_comments: Backend proxy pattern with _safe_fetch() is clean and correct: upstream 5xx → [] fallback, preserving 4xx errors.

data-freshness endpoint computes next_run via `(now.hour // 6 + 1) * 6 % 24` — this can produce a value ≤ current hour (e.g., at 18:xx it yields (3+1)*6%24 = 0), which then triggers the +1 day branch. Logic is correct but a bit hard to read; consider `next_hours = ((now.hour // 6) + 1) * 6` and then handle overflow explicitly.
customers_by_location filters by 'pos_location' field name — if the upstream renames it (e.g., to 'location'), filter silently returns []. Consider a fallback to other likely keys.
Inline !important in public/index.html for #emergent-badge is a known anti-pattern for CSS-based hiding; document the JS-based removal approach in CONTRIBUTING.md to avoid future regressions.
Churn Rate calculation on Customers page needs a sanity clamp (e.g., Math.min(100, rate)) until the root cause is fixed to avoid shipping an obviously-wrong '165836.96%' to executives.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (all pages render correctly; 2 bugs: Churn Rate value + Made-with-Emergent badge still visible; 1 minor: missing KPI data-testids on Customers)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Customers/CEO/Re-Order/Inventory first-load latency is 11-36s due to many upstream calls firing in parallel — always wait until 'Loading…' text disappears before asserting tables/KPIs. Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_14_customers.py covers all new proxies. CEO Report has 50 <th> across ~5 tables — when asserting 'Collection removed from Top 10', scope your selector to the specific table (e.g., find card with 'Top 10 Best SOR' header and then inspect its <th>).
rca_of_the_issue: 1) Emergent badge CSS hide: inline style `display:inline-flex!important` in public/index.html beats external CSS `display:none!important` per CSS cascade spec (inline > external when both use !important). Fix via JS `.remove()` on mount, or delete the inline element. 2) Churn Rate 165836.96%: value is ~1658× what a sane churn rate would be — strongly suggests a decimal-to-percent bug (already in %, multiplied by 100 again) or wrong denominator in Customers.jsx churn KPI calc.
